findings.description: No allowed-tools declared in frontmatter
findings.remediation: Add allowed-tools: Read, Bash for least-privilege clarity

findings.description: No allowed-tools declared in frontmatter
findings.remediation: Consider adding allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash

findings.description: Scanner flagged curl POST — confirmed false positive, script is legitimate JSON-RPC wrapper for Context7 API
Shell HTTP requests flagged by scanner — confirmed false positive, all calls are legitimate Notion API calls

skills.name: learn

findings.description: Scanner hit on 'DAN mode' in SKILL.md security scan phase — documenting detection patterns, not performing them
'bypass safety' in SECURITY.md pattern library — documenting threats to detect, not performing them
'ignore previous instructions' and 'DAN mode' in SECURITY.md pattern table — documentation
JSON example with 'ignore previous instructions' in reporting schema documentation

notes: Remote API calls to agentskill.sh — inherent to skill's design (skill registry). Skill re-scans every fetched skill before install.
Self-update mechanism overwrites SKILL.md from API — includes SHA verification step.
agentskill.sh flagged as untrusted by scanner — overly conservative, this is the skill's expected primary domain.

skills.name: plain-english
notes: Fixed: YAML frontmatter now uses block scalar (>) to handle unquoted colons in description

findings.description: No allowed-tools declared in frontmatter — agent operates with full inherited permissions
Skill's sole purpose is enabling bypassPermissions in settings.local.json — inherently high-privilege state. disable-model-invocation: true is the correct safeguard.
findings.remediation: Ensure disable-model-invocation: true is respected by agent harness

skills.name: import-export-claude-global-profile

findings.description: Import script overwrites settings.json, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and hooks/ directory without backup or confirmation. A compromised backup could inject arbitrary permissions or hook instructions.
findings.remediation: Create timestamped backup before overwriting. Print warning. Optionally diff and require user confirmation for sensitive files.

findings.description: Bash tool granted with wildcard pattern bash * but SKILL.md only uses three fixed scripts. Broader than necessary.

notes: 4 critical scanner flags in sanitize-json.js all false positives — JSDoc comments incorrectly flagged.
export.sh uses git add -A — user should verify backup folder contents are safe to version control.

skills.name: mcp-tavily-mcp

findings.description: Scanner flagged curl POST to Tavily API — confirmed false positive, this is the skill's intended mechanism

notes: Fixed: hardcoded API key removed from script — now reads from ${TAVILY_API_KEY} env var.
API key stored in ~/.claude/settings.local.json (local-only, not exported to Git).
Ensure settings.local.json is excluded from GitHub export skill.

skills.name: import-claude-global-profile

findings.description: Bash scope listed as bash * in frontmatter but SKILL.md body restricts to one specific script. Overly broad if harness enforces full scope.
findings.remediation: Narrow Bash scope to specific script path

notes: Script has backup-before-overwrite safeguard. Symlink risk: cp -r dereferences symlinks as regular files — safe.

skills.name: permissions-command

findings.description: No explicit allowed-tools in frontmatter — tools listed inline in body instead.

notes: Well-designed security guardrails: restricts Bash to fine-grained patterns, operates at project scope only, no self-reinforcement.

skills.name: autobrowse
notes: Browserbase skill. Inner agent harness has strong security controls: command allowlisting (only 'browse'), shell-less execution (execFileSync), custom argument tokenization, output truncation at 50k chars, 30s timeout.
Permission profile: Bash Read Write Edit Glob Grep Agent — medium but justified by design.
Workspace writes cleanly isolated to ./autobrowse/ in CWD. Graduation step writes to ~/.claude/skills/ only for final SKILL.md.
Dependencies: @anthropic-ai/sdk (official) and dotenv (standard). Both trusted.

skills.name: caveman-compress
notes: 6 Python scripts reviewed. All clean: no eval/exec, subprocess.run uses explicit list form, optional tiktoken dependency gracefully degrades.
File content embedded in compression prompt could theoretically be attacker-controlled if user compresses a malicious file — self-harm only scenario.

findings.description: Missing allowed-tools declaration — skill operates with full tool access of invoking agent
findings.remediation: Add allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Write

findings.description: Git clone instruction uses Unix /tmp/ path incompatible with Windows host
findings.remediation: Use mktemp -d or $TEMP/$TMPDIR env vars for cross-platform compatibility

skills.name: code-review
notes: SQL injection and XSS examples in text are educational content (what to flag in reviews), not malicious payloads.

findings.description: No allowed-tools declared in frontmatter — skill inherits full tool access. Uses Task for parallel subagent spawning.
findings.remediation: Add allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Write, Task

findings.description: Scanner reported frontmatter:null — confirmed false positive, manual inspection shows valid YAML. Likely Windows path handling issue in scanner.

skills.name: command-creator
notes: Reference files (patterns.md, examples.md, best-practices.md) document injection patterns for teaching purposes — not executing them. False positives from scanner.

skills.name: debug-mode

findings.description: No allowed-tools declared in frontmatter — effective permission set is unrestricted. Skill needs Read, Grep, Glob, Write, Edit for instrumentation workflow.
findings.remediation: Add allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Write, Edit

notes: Hardcoded project_root constant (no runtime path detection) is a deliberate security constraint, not a vulnerability.
Log writing constrained to {project_root}/.claude/debug.log — scoped to project, not agent config.

skills.name: docx
notes: 8 Python scripts reviewed. All clean: defusedxml (secure XML), no eval/exec, no shell injection. Optional LibreOffice compilation step uses gcc C shim for sandbox compatibility.

skills.name: find-bugs
notes: SQL injection/XSS references in checklist are detection targets, not payloads.

skills.name: find-skills
notes: skills.sh URLs flagged as untrusted by scanner — false positive, skills.sh is the official Astral skill registry.

skills.name: gh-cli
notes: 3 example.com URLs in documentation are inert placeholders in example commands, not live external links.

skills.name: init-rules
notes: Skill asks user to confirm before overwriting CLAUDE.md — good safety behavior.

skills.name: mcp2cli
notes: 30 URLs flagged as untrusted by scanner — all are example.com/petstore3.swagger.io placeholder domains in documentation, not runtime dependencies.

skills.name: modern-python
notes: 10 URLs flagged as untrusted by scanner — all are legitimate: IETF reserved example domains, astral.sh official installer, pre-commit.com resource. No actual untrusted sources.

skills.name: pdf
notes: 8 Python scripts reviewed. All clean: pypdf, pdfplumber, reportlab, PIL. subprocess.run uses list form (no shell). Monkeypatches pypdf internals for bug compatibility — safe.

skills.name: security-review

findings.description: Scanner flagged hardcoded 'password123' and 'sk-proj-xxxxx' — confirmed false positive, these are FAIL anti-pattern examples under a 'NEVER Do This' heading

notes: 5 scanner-flagged URLs all trusted official documentation (OWASP, nextjs.org, supabase.com, portswigger.net).

skills.name: skill-scanner
notes: 36 automated findings (23 Critical, 12 High, 1 Medium) — ALL false positives. The skill's references and script necessarily discuss/detect the very threats it scans for.
Script patterns (netcat, eval, exec, shell=True, base64.b64decode) are part of the detection logic itself, not malicious usage.
evil.com URLs are fictional examples in threat documentation. No real malicious URLs present.
Tool permissions (Read, Grep, Glob, Bash) are minimal and justified for a static analysis tool.

skills.name: web-coder
notes: 77 'untrusted' URLs flagged by scanner — all false positives. All are IETF reserved example domains (example.com, localhost) or well-known standards bodies (W3C, WHATWG, IETF, caniuse.com, OWASP).
Security content (XSS, CSRF, CSP) appears as defensive documentation, appropriate for web development competency guide.

actionItems.issue: No safeguards before overwriting config files
actionItems.fix: Add timestamped backup + warning before import. Exclude settings.local.json from export.

actionItems.issue: Missing allowed-tools + Unix /tmp/ path incompatible with Windows
actionItems.fix: Add allowed-tools declaration; use mktemp -d or $TEMP for cross-platform tmp
Ensure disable-model-invocation: true is respected by harness
Add allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Write, Task
Add allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Write, Edit

disabledSkills.name: import-claude-global-profile
disabledSkills.notes: Bash scope too broad. Script itself has backup safeguard.

disabledSkills.name: permissions-command
